steps.title: Graph the equation directly
steps.content:
For an absolute-value equation with answer choices, graph both sides in Desmos:
$$y=-4|5x+6|+4$$
$$y=-20$$
The intersections give all solutions.

steps.title: Read the intersections
steps.content: The graph shows two intersections: $x=0$ and $x=-\frac{12}{5}$. That already matches choice C.

steps.title: Confirm by isolating the absolute value
steps.content:
Subtract $4$ and divide by $-4$:
$$|5x+6|=6$$
So $5x+6=6$ or $5x+6=-6$, giving $x=0$ or $x=-\frac{12}{5}$. Choice C is correct.
